evals.prompt: Run the review panel on this spec
evals.expected_output: Multi-perspective review with genuine persona diversity, specific references, and comprehensive coverage of the date filtering spec.
expectations: Persona fidelity: Junior dev (Alex) asks implementation/clarity questions; Staff eng (Morgan) raises architecture/security/scalability; PO (Jordan) flags acceptance criteria gaps and completeness; End user (Sam) flags expectation mismatches or UX confusion
Unique contributions: At least 2 reviewers surface substantive issues that no other reviewer raised — proving genuine perspective diversity, not 4 copies of the same review
Specificity: Review points reference specific sections, quote from the document, or give concrete examples — not vague 'this could be clearer' feedback
Coverage breadth: Review collectively covers at least 4 issue categories (e.g., clarity/ambiguity, architecture/technical, completeness/scope, usability/expectations)
Non-prioritization: Summary preserves all substantive feedback from individual reviews — the summarizer didn't silently discard anything meaningful (minor noise annotation is fine)
Flags the undecided configuration mechanism (TBD) as a gap or concern
Notes the naive month/year approximation (30 days = 1 month) as a potential issue

evals.prompt: I want fresh eyes on the architecture doc — what am I missing?
evals.expected_output: Multi-perspective review adapted to architecture doc context, with genuine persona diversity and specific, actionable feedback.
expectations: Persona fidelity: Each reviewer demonstrates their specific lens — junior dev flags confusing parts, staff eng raises design concerns, PO flags completeness, end user adapts to developer/architecture consumer perspective
Unique contributions: At least 2 reviewers surface substantive issues that no other reviewer raised
Specificity: Review points reference specific sections, quote from the document, or give concrete examples
Coverage breadth: Review collectively covers at least 4 issue categories
Non-prioritization: Summary preserves all substantive feedback without silent filtering

evals.prompt: Is this ready to implement? Poke holes in it.
evals.expected_output: Multi-perspective review catching the deliberate gaps in this short spec, with each persona contributing distinct types of feedback.
expectations: Persona fidelity: Each reviewer demonstrates their specific lens appropriate to the document type
Unique contributions: At least 2 reviewers surface substantive issues that no other reviewer raised
Specificity: Review points reference specific sections, quote from the document, or give concrete examples
Coverage breadth: Review collectively covers at least 4 issue categories
Non-prioritization: Summary preserves all substantive feedback without silent filtering
Flags undefined failure/error handling semantics (what happens when one task in the batch fails)
Flags 'performance is acceptable' as vague or unmeasurable
Notes the response format/structure is undefined
